docs.title: IRB Interface
docs.text:
Virtual Networks → IIIRB Interfaces

Documentation coming soon!

docs.title: Custom Resource Definition
docs.text:
To browse the Custom Resource Definition go to crd.eda.dev.

IRBInterface

services.eda.nokia.com / v2

Expand All

SPEC

The IRBInterface enables the configuration and management of Layer 3 interfaces associated with a BridgeDomain. This resource allows for the specification of various parameters, including IP MTU, learning of unsolicited ARPs, IPv4 and IPv6 addresses, and unnumbered interface settings. It also supports advanced features such as BFD configuration, Virtual IP discovery, and ARP/ND-related settings like Proxy ARP/ND and EVPN route advertisement.

  • anycastGatewayMAC string #

    The gateway MAC to use on the anycast address, if left empty the node will automatically assign one.

  • bfd object #

    Enable BFD on the IRBInterface.

    • desiredMinTransmitIntMs integer #

      The minimum interval in milliseconds between transmission of BFD control packets.

      default: 1000range: 10 to 100000
    • detectionMultiplier integer #

      The number of packets that must be missed to declare this session as down.

      default: 3range: 3 to 20
    • enabled* boolean #

      Enables Biforward Detection.

      default: false
    • requiredMinEchoReceiveIntMs integer #

      The minimum interval between echo packets the local node can receive.

      default: 0range: 0 to 100000
    • requiredMinReceiveIntMs integer #

      The minimum interval in milliseconds between received BFD control packets that this system should support.

      default: 1000range: 10 to 100000
    • ttl integer #

      Sets custom IP TTL or Hop Limit for multi-hop BFD sessions packets. Not applicable to single-hop BFD sessions.

      range: 2 to 255
  • bridgeDomain* string #

    Reference to a BridgeDomain.

  • description string #

    The description of the IRBInterface.

  • egress object #

    Manages actions on traffic at Egress.

    • filters array[string] #

      List of Filter references to use at egress.

    • qosPolicies array[string] #

      List of QoS Egress policy references to use at egress.

  • evpnRouteAdvertisementType object #

    Controls the type of ARP/ND entries to advertise.

    • arpDynamic boolean #

      Advertise dynamic ARP entries.

      default: false
    • arpStatic boolean #

      Advertise static ARP entries.

      default: false
    • ndDynamic boolean #

      Advertise dynamic ND entries.

      default: false
    • ndStatic boolean #

      Advertise static ND entries.

      default: false
    • rfc9135SymmetricMode boolean #

      Use RFC9135-based symmetric mode for ARP/ND host route advertisements.

  • hostRoutePopulate object #

    Configures host route population based on ARP entries.

    • dynamic object #

      Create host routes out of dynamic ARP/ND entries.

      • datapathProgramming boolean #

        Enable datapath programming for host routes.

      • populate* boolean #

        Enable population of host routes based on ARP/ND entries.

        default: false
    • evpn object #

      Create host routes out of EVPN learned ARP/ND entries.

      • datapathProgramming boolean #

        Enable datapath programming for host routes.

      • populate* boolean #

        Enable population of host routes based on ARP/ND entries.

        default: false
    • static object #

      Create host routes out of static ARP/ND entries.

      • datapathProgramming boolean #

        Enable datapath programming for host routes.

      • populate* boolean #

        Enable population of host routes based on ARP/ND entries.

        default: false
  • ingress object #

    Manages actions on traffic at Ingress.

    • filters array[string] #

      List of Filter references to use at ingress.

    • qosPolicies array[string] #

      List of QoS Ingress policy references to use at ingress.

  • ipAddresses array[object] #

    Configures IP addresses on the IRBInterface.

    • [] object #
      • ipv4Address object #

        IPv4 address in IP/mask form, e.g., 192.168.0.1/24.

        • anycast boolean #

          Indicates if the address is an anycast IRB address.

        • ipPrefix* string #

          IP Address and prefix length in CIDR notation.

        • primary boolean #

          Indicates which address to use as primary.

      • ipv6Address object #

        IPv6 address in IP/mask form, e.g., fc00::1/120.

        • anycast boolean #

          Indicates if the address is an anycast IRB address.

        • ipPrefix* string #

          IP Address and prefix length in CIDR notation.

        • primary boolean #

          Indicates which address to use as primary.

      • node string #

        Reference to a TopoNode resource, if not specified the IP address will be assigned to all nodes on which the IRB is deployed. If specified the IP address will be assigned to the specified node.

  • ipMTU integer #

    IP MTU for the IRBInterface [default=1500].

    default: 1500range: 1280 to 9486
  • ipv4 object #

    IPv4 parameters that are not applicable to IPv6.

    • arpTimeoutSeconds integer #

      Duration of time that dynamic ARP entries remain in the ARP cache before they expire.

      default: 14400format: int32range: >= 0
    • directedBroadcast boolean #

      Allow receiving and forwarding of directed broadcast packets. Enabled when set to true.

    • learnUnsolicitedARP string #

      Enable or disable learning of unsolicited ARPs.

      default: "Disabled"enum: "Enabled", "Disabled"
  • ipv6 object #

    IPv6 parameters that are not applicable to IPv4.

    • learnUnsolicitedNA string #

      Enable or disable learning of unsolicited Neighbor Advertisements.

      default: "Disabled"enum: "All", "Global", "LinkLocal", "Disabled"
    • routerAdvertisement object #

      Manages IPv6 Router Advertisement parameters.

      • currentHopLimit* integer #

        The current hop limit to advertise in the router advertisement messages.

        default: 64format: int32range: 0 to 255
      • enabled* boolean #

        Enable or disable IPv6 router advertisements.

        default: false
      • ipMTU integer #

        The IP MTU to advertise in the router advertisement messages.

        format: int32range: 1280 to 9486
      • managedConfigurationFlag* boolean #

        Enable DHCPv6 for address configuration (M-bit).

        default: false
      • maxAdvertisementIntervalSeconds* integer #

        Maximum time between router advertisements (in seconds).

        default: 600format: int32range: 4 to 1800
      • minAdvertisementIntervalSeconds* integer #

        Minimum time between router advertisements (in seconds).

        default: 200format: int32range: 3 to 1350
      • otherConfigurationFlag* boolean #

        Enable DHCPv6 for other configuration (O-bit).

        default: false
      • prefixes array[object] #

        IPv6 prefixes to advertise in router advertisements.

        • [] object #

          IPv6Prefix defines the configuration for an IPv6 prefix advertisement.

          • autonomousFlag boolean #

            When this is set in the prefix information option hosts can use the prefix for stateless address autoconfiguration (SLAAC).

            default: true
          • onLinkFlag boolean #

            When this is set in the prefix information option hosts can use the prefix for on-link determination.

            default: true
          • preferredLifetimeSeconds integer #

            The length of time in seconds (relative to the time the packet is sent) that addresses generated from the prefix via stateless address autoconfiguration remain preferred.

            default: 604800format: int32range: 0 to 4294967295
          • prefix* string #

            An IPv6 global unicast address prefix.

          • validLifetimeSeconds integer #

            The length of time in seconds (relative to the time the packet is sent) that the prefix is valid for the purpose of on-link determination.

            default: 2592000format: int32range: 0 to 4294967295
      • reachableTimeMs integer #

        Time in milliseconds for Neighbor Unreachability Detection.

        default: 0format: int32range: 0 to 3600000
      • retransmitTimeMs* integer #

        Time in milliseconds between retransmitted NS messages.

        default: 0format: int32range: 0 to 1800000
      • routerLifetimeSeconds* integer #

        Router lifetime in seconds for default gateway.

        default: 1800format: int32range: 0 to 9000
  • l3ProxyARPND object #

    L3 Proxy ARP and ND configuration.

    • proxyARP boolean #

      Select whether Proxy ARP should be enabled.

      default: false
    • proxyND boolean #

      Select whether Proxy ND should be enabled.

      default: false
  • router* string #

    Reference to a Router.

  • virtualIPDiscovery array[object] #

    Configuration for Virtual IP discovery on the interface.

    • [] object #
      • address* string #

        Virtual IP Address.

      • allowedMACs array[string] #

        List of allowed MAC addresses for a discovered virtual IP address.

      • bridgeInterfaces array[string] #

        List of BridgeInterfaces on the associated MAC-VRF to which the ARP probes are sent. If left blank, the probes are sent on all BridgeInterfaces associated with the BridgeDomain.

      • probeIntervalSeconds integer #

        ARP probe interval in seconds.

        default: 0range: 0 to 86400
      • vlans array[string] #

        List of VLANs on the associated BridgeDomain to which the ARP probes are sent. If left blank, the probes are sent on all VLANs associated with the BridgeDomain.

STATUS

IRBInterfaceStatus defines the observed state of IRBInterface

  • health integer #

    Indicates the health score of the VNET.

  • healthScoreReason string #

    Explains the reason for the health score.

  • interfaces array[object] #

    Details of the interfaces associated with the IRB.

    • [] object #
      • enabled boolean #

        Administrative status of the SubInterface.

      • ipv4Addresses array[object] #

        List of IPv4 addresses

        • [] object #
          • anycast boolean #

            Indicates if the address is an anycast IRB address.

          • ipPrefix* string #

            IP Address and prefix length in CIDR notation.

          • primary boolean #

            Indicates which address to use as primary.

      • ipv6Addresses array[object] #

        List of IPv6 addresses

        • [] object #
          • anycast boolean #

            Indicates if the address is an anycast IRB address.

          • ipPrefix* string #

            IP Address and prefix length in CIDR notation.

          • primary boolean #

            Indicates which address to use as primary.

      • lastChangeTime string #

        Timestamp of when this SubInterface last changed state.

      • node* string #

        Reference to Node object.

      • nodeInterface* string #

        Node specific interface name.

      • operatingSystem string #

        Operating System of the Node.

      • operationalState string #

        Current operational state of the SubInterface.

        enum: "Up", "Down", "Degraded", "Unknown"
  • lastChangeTime string #

    Timestamp of the last state change.

  • operationalState string #

    Operational state of the VNET.

    enum: "Up", "Down", "Degraded", "Unknown"

docs.title: Custom Resource Definition
docs.text:
To browse the Custom Resource Definition go to crd.eda.dev.

RoutedInterface

services.eda.nokia.com / v2

Expand All

SPEC

The RoutedInterface enables the configuration and management of Layer 3 interfaces for routing traffic between different networks. This resource allows for specifying an underlying Interface and Router, configuring VLAN IDs, and setting the IP MTU. It also supports the learning of unsolicited ARPs, defining both IPv4 and IPv6 addresses, and enabling unnumbered interfaces. Advanced features such as BFD configuration, Proxy ARP/ND, and ARP timeout settings are included to ensure robust and efficient routing.

  • bfd object #

    Enables BFD on the RoutedInterface.

    • desiredMinTransmitIntMs integer #

      The minimum interval in milliseconds between transmission of BFD control packets.

      default: 1000range: 10 to 100000
    • detectionMultiplier integer #

      The number of packets that must be missed to declare this session as down.

      default: 3range: 3 to 20
    • enabled* boolean #

      Enables Biforward Detection.

      default: false
    • requiredMinEchoReceiveIntMs integer #

      The minimum interval between echo packets the local node can receive.

      default: 0range: 0 to 100000
    • requiredMinReceiveIntMs integer #

      The minimum interval in milliseconds between received BFD control packets that this system should support.

      default: 1000range: 10 to 100000
    • ttl integer #

      Sets custom IP TTL or Hop Limit for multi-hop BFD sessions packets. Not applicable to single-hop BFD sessions.

      range: 2 to 255
  • description string #

    The description of the RoutedInterface.

  • egress object #

    Manages actions on traffic at Egress.

    • filters array[string] #

      List of Filter references to use at egress.

    • qosPolicies array[string] #

      List of QoS Egress policy references to use at egress.

  • hostRoutePopulate object #

    Configures host route population based on ARP/ND entries.

    • dynamic object #

      Create host routes out of dynamic ARP/ND entries.

      • datapathProgramming boolean #

        Enable datapath programming for host routes.

      • populate* boolean #

        Enable population of host routes based on ARP/ND entries.

        default: false
    • evpn object #

      Create host routes out of EVPN learned ARP/ND entries.

      • datapathProgramming boolean #

        Enable datapath programming for host routes.

      • populate* boolean #

        Enable population of host routes based on ARP/ND entries.

        default: false
    • static object #

      Create host routes out of static ARP/ND entries.

      • datapathProgramming boolean #

        Enable datapath programming for host routes.

      • populate* boolean #

        Enable population of host routes based on ARP/ND entries.

        default: false
  • ingress object #

    Manages actions on traffic at Ingress.

    • filters array[string] #

      List of Filter references to use at ingress.

    • qosPolicies array[string] #

      List of QoS Ingress policy references to use at ingress.

  • interface* string #

    Reference to an Interface to use for attachment.

  • ipMTU integer #

    IP MTU for the RoutedInterface.

    default: 1500range: 1280 to 9486
  • ipv4 object #

    IPv4 parameters that are not applicable to IPv6.

    • arpTimeoutSeconds integer #

      Duration of time that dynamic ARP entries remain in the ARP cache before they expire.

      default: 14400format: int32range: >= 0
    • directedBroadcast boolean #

      Allow receiving and forwarding of directed broadcast packets. Enabled when set to true.

    • learnUnsolicitedARP string #

      Enable or disable learning of unsolicited ARPs.

      default: "Disabled"enum: "Enabled", "Disabled"
  • ipv4Addresses array[object] #

    List of IPv4 addresses in IP/mask form, e.g., 192.168.0.1/24.

    • [] object #
      • ipPrefix* string #

        IP Address and prefix length in CIDR notation.

      • primary boolean #

        Indicates which address to use as primary.

  • ipv6 object #

    IPv6 parameters that are not applicable to IPv4.

    • learnUnsolicitedNA string #

      Enable or disable learning of unsolicited Neighbor Advertisements.

      default: "Disabled"enum: "All", "Global", "LinkLocal", "Disabled"
    • routerAdvertisement object #

      Manages IPv6 Router Advertisement parameters.

      • currentHopLimit* integer #

        The current hop limit to advertise in the router advertisement messages.

        default: 64format: int32range: 0 to 255
      • enabled* boolean #

        Enable or disable IPv6 router advertisements.

        default: false
      • ipMTU integer #

        The IP MTU to advertise in the router advertisement messages.

        format: int32range: 1280 to 9486
      • managedConfigurationFlag* boolean #

        Enable DHCPv6 for address configuration (M-bit).

        default: false
      • maxAdvertisementIntervalSeconds* integer #

        Maximum time between router advertisements (in seconds).

        default: 600format: int32range: 4 to 1800
      • minAdvertisementIntervalSeconds* integer #

        Minimum time between router advertisements (in seconds).

        default: 200format: int32range: 3 to 1350
      • otherConfigurationFlag* boolean #

        Enable DHCPv6 for other configuration (O-bit).

        default: false
      • prefixes array[object] #

        IPv6 prefixes to advertise in router advertisements.

        • [] object #

          IPv6Prefix defines the configuration for an IPv6 prefix advertisement.

          • autonomousFlag boolean #

            When this is set in the prefix information option hosts can use the prefix for stateless address autoconfiguration (SLAAC).

            default: true
          • onLinkFlag boolean #

            When this is set in the prefix information option hosts can use the prefix for on-link determination.

            default: true
          • preferredLifetimeSeconds integer #

            The length of time in seconds (relative to the time the packet is sent) that addresses generated from the prefix via stateless address autoconfiguration remain preferred.

            default: 604800format: int32range: 0 to 4294967295
          • prefix* string #

            An IPv6 global unicast address prefix.

          • validLifetimeSeconds integer #

            The length of time in seconds (relative to the time the packet is sent) that the prefix is valid for the purpose of on-link determination.

            default: 2592000format: int32range: 0 to 4294967295
      • reachableTimeMs integer #

        Time in milliseconds for Neighbor Unreachability Detection.

        default: 0format: int32range: 0 to 3600000
      • retransmitTimeMs* integer #

        Time in milliseconds between retransmitted NS messages.

        default: 0format: int32range: 0 to 1800000
      • routerLifetimeSeconds* integer #

        Router lifetime in seconds for default gateway.

        default: 1800format: int32range: 0 to 9000
  • ipv6Addresses array[object] #

    List of IPv6 addresses in IP/mask form, e.g., fc00::1/120.

    • [] object #
      • ipPrefix* string #

        IP Address and prefix length in CIDR notation.

      • primary boolean #

        Indicates which address to use as primary.

  • l3ProxyARPND object #

    L3 Proxy ARP and ND configuration.

    • proxyARP boolean #

      Select whether Proxy ARP should be enabled.

      default: false
    • proxyND boolean #

      Select whether Proxy ND should be enabled.

      default: false
  • router* string #

    Reference to a Router.

  • vlanID string #

    Single value between 1-4094 support, ranges supported in the format x-y,x-y, or the special keyword null, any, untagged or pool for auto allocation.

    default: "pool"
  • vlanPool string #

    Reference to a VLAN pool to use for allocations.

    default: "vlan-pool"

STATUS

RoutedInterfaceStatus defines the observed state of RoutedInterface

  • health integer #

    Indicates the health score of the RoutedInterface.

  • healthScoreReason string #

    Indicates the reason for the health score.

  • lastChangeTime string #

    The time when the state of the resource last changed.

  • operationalState string #

    Operational state of the RoutedInterface.

    enum: "Up", "Down", "Degraded", "Unknown"
  • subinterfaces array[object] #

    Sub-interface status within the RoutedInterface.

    • [] object #
      • enabled boolean #

        The administrative status of the SubInterface.

      • interface* string #

        Normalized interface name.

      • interfaceResource* string #

        Eda interface resource.

      • lastChangeTime string #

        Indicates when this SubInterface last changed state.

      • node* string #

        Reference to Node object.

      • nodeInterface* string #

        Node specific interface name.

      • operatingSystem string #

        Operating System of the Node.

      • operationalState string #

        Indicates the current operational state of the SubInterface.

        enum: "Up", "Down", "Degraded", "Unknown"
      • subinterfaceIndex integer #

        Index allocated to the subinterface on the associated interface for the given VLAN.

      • vlanID string #

        vlan assigned to this subinterface.

docs.title: Custom Resource Definition
docs.text:
To browse the Custom Resource Definition go to crd.eda.dev.

Router

services.eda.nokia.com / v2

Expand All

SPEC

The Router enables the configuration and management of routing functions within a network. This resource allows for setting a unique Router ID, configuring VNIs and EVIs with options for automatic allocation, and defining import and export route targets. It also includes advanced configuration options such as BGP settings, including autonomous system numbers, AFI/SAFI options, and route advertisement preferences. Node selectors can be used to constrain the deployment of the router to specific nodes within the network.

  • bgp object #

    BGP configuration.

    • autonomousSystem integer #

      Autonomous System number for BGP.

      range: 1 to 4294967295
    • ebgpPreference integer #

      Preference to be set for eBGP [default=170].

      default: 170range: 1 to 255
    • enabled boolean #

      Enable or disable BGP.

      default: false
    • exportPolicies array[string] #

      Reference to a Policy CR that will be used to filter routes advertised to peers.

    • ibgpPreference integer #

      Preference to be set for iBGP [default=170].

      default: 170range: 1 to 255
    • importPolicies array[string] #

      Reference to a Policy CR that will be used to filter routes received from peers.

    • ipAliasNexthops array[object] #

      IP aliasing configuration.

      • [] object #
        • esi string #

          10 byte Ethernet Segment Identifier, if not set a type 0 ESI is generated.

          default: "auto"
        • nextHop* string #

          The nexthop IP address to track for the IP alias.

        • preferredActiveNode string #

          When not set the ES is used in an all active mode. This references the TopoNode object and when set, the DF algorithm is configured to type preference and the selected Node is set with a higher preference value. All other Nodes have a lower value configured.

    • ipv4Unicast object #

      Parameters relating to the IPv4 unicast AFI/SAFI.

      • advertiseIPv6NextHops boolean #

        Enables advertisement of IPv4 Unicast routes with IPv6 next-hops to peers.

      • enabled* boolean #

        Enables the IPv4 unicast AFISAFI.

        default: false
      • multipath object #

        Enable multipath.

        • allowMultipleAS* boolean #

          When set to true, BGP is allowed to build a multipath set using BGP routes with different neighbor AS (most recent AS in the AS_PATH), When set to false, BGP is only allowed to use non-best paths for ECMP if they meet the multipath criteria and they have the same neighbor AS as the best path.

          default: true
        • maxAllowedPaths* integer #

          The maximum number of BGP ECMP next-hops for BGP routes with an NLRI belonging to the address family of this configuration context.

          range: 1 to 256
      • receiveIPv6NextHops boolean #

        Enables the advertisement of the RFC 5549 capability to receive IPv4 routes with IPv6 next-hops.

    • ipv6Unicast object #

      Parameters relating to the IPv6 unicast AFI/SAFI.

      • enabled* boolean #

        Enables the IPv6 unicast AFISAFI

        default: false
      • multipath object #

        Enable multipath

        • allowMultipleAS* boolean #

          When set to true, BGP is allowed to build a multipath set using BGP routes with different neighbor AS (most recent AS in the AS_PATH), When set to false, BGP is only allowed to use non-best paths for ECMP if they meet the multipath criteria and they have the same neighbor AS as the best path.

          default: true
        • maxAllowedPaths* integer #

          The maximum number of BGP ECMP next-hops for BGP routes with an NLRI belonging to the address family of this configuration context.

          range: 1 to 256
    • keychain string #

      Keychain to be used for authentication

    • minWaitToAdvertiseSeconds integer #

      Minimum wait time before advertising routes post BGP restart.

      default: 0range: 0 to 3600
    • rapidWithdrawl boolean #

      Enable rapid withdrawal in BGP.

      default: true
    • waitForFIBInstall boolean #

      Wait for FIB installation before advertising routes.

      default: false
  • configuredName string #

    The name of the Router to configure on the device.

  • description string #

    The description of the Router.

  • ecmp integer #

    Set the maximum number of ECMP paths for the Router. This is supported only by some platforms, and will be ignored for others.

    range: 1 to 256
  • encapOptions object #

    Encapsulation-specific options (such as MPLS tunneling options).

    default: {"vxlan": {"tunnelIndexPool": "tunnel-index-pool", "vniPool": "vni-pool"}}
    • mpls object #

      MPLS encapsulation optionss. Only applicable if MPLS encapsulation is used (Type=EVPNMPLS or Type=IPVPN).

      • allowedTunnelTypes array[string] #

        Allowed tunnel types for MPLS transport.

    • vxlan object #

      VXLAN encapsulation options. Only applicable if VXLAN encapsulation is used (Type=EVPNVXLAN).

      • tunnelIndexPool string #

        Reference to tunnel index allocation pool.

        default: "tunnel-index-pool"
      • vni integer #

        VNI for the Router; leave blank for auto-allocation from VNI pool. Only applicable to VXLAN encapsulation.

        range: 1 to 16777215
      • vniPool string #

        Reference to VNI pool for auto-allocation. Only applicable to VXLAN encapsulation.

        default: "vni-pool"
  • evi integer #

    EVI for the Router; leave blank for auto-allocation from EVI pool. Optional for IPVPN (can be used to auto-derive RTs), required for EVPN.

    range: 1 to 65535
  • eviPool string #

    Reference to EVI pool for auto-allocation.

    default: "evi-pool"
  • exportTarget string #

    Export route target in 'target:N:N' format, if not specified, the default value taken as "target:1:<evi>".

  • importTarget string #

    Import route target in 'target:N:N' format, if not specified, the default value taken as "target:1:<evi>".

  • nodeSelectors array[string] #

    Node selectors for deployment constraints. If Nodes are selected, the Router will only be deployed on the Nodes selected, if left blank it will be deployed on all Nodes for which there are IRB or RoutedInterfaces referencing this Router.

  • resilientHashing object #

    Resilient Hashing configuration.

    • prefixes array[object] #

      IPv4 or IPv6 prefix. Active routes in the FIB that exactly match this prefix or that are longer matches of this prefix are provided with resilient-hash programming.

      • [] object #
        • hashBucketsPerPath* integer #

          The number of times each next-hop is repeated in the fill pattern if there are max-paths ECMP next-hops.

          default: 1range: 1 to 32
        • maxECMP* integer #

          The maximum number of ECMP next-hops per route associated with the resilient-hash prefix.

          default: 1range: 1 to 64
        • prefix* string #

          IPv4 or IPv6 prefix. Active routes in the FIB that exactly match this prefix or that are longer matches of this prefix are provided with resilient-hash programming.

  • routeLeaking object #

    Route leaking controlled by routing policies in and out of the DefaultRouter.

    • exportPolicy string #

      Reference to a Policy resource to use when evaluating route exports from the DefaultRouter.

    • importPolicy string #

      Reference to a Policy resource to use when evaluating route imports into the DefaultRouter.

  • routerID string #

    Router ID.

  • type string #

    Select the type of Router. Simple doesn't include any overlay control plane or dataplane properties (such as EVPN/VXLAN). EVPNVXLAN includes the properties needed to provision this Router over an IP Fabric.

    default: "EVPNVXLAN"enum: "Simple", "EVPNVXLAN", "EVPNMPLS", "IPVPN"

STATUS

RouterStatus defines the observed state of Router

  • bgpPeers array[string] #

    List of BGPPeers attached to the router.

  • evi integer #

    EVI in use for this Router.

  • exportTarget string #

    Export route target for this Router.

  • health integer #

    Indicates the health score of the Router.

  • healthScoreReason string #

    Explains the reason for the health score.

  • importTarget string #

    Import route target for this Router.

  • irbInterfaces array[string] #

    List of IRBInterfaces attached to the router.

  • lastChangeTime string #

    Timestamp of the last state change.

  • nodes array[string] #

    List of nodes on which the Router is deployed.

  • numNodes integer #

    Number of nodes on which the Router is configured.

  • operationalState string #

    Operational state of the Router.

    enum: "Up", "Down", "Degraded", "Unknown"
  • ospfInstances array[object] #

    List of OSPFInstances configured on the router.

    • [] object #
      • areas array[object] #
        • [] object #
          • id* string #
          • interfaces array[string] #
      • name* string #
      • version string #
  • routedInterfaces array[string] #

    List of RoutedInterfaces attached to the router.

  • tunnelIndex integer #

    Vxlan tunnel index in use.

  • vni integer #

    VNI in use for this Router.

docs.title: Custom Resource Definition
docs.text:
To browse the Custom Resource Definition go to crd.eda.dev.

RouterInterconnect

services.eda.nokia.com / v2

Expand All

SPEC

RouterInterconnectSpec allows to configure a RouterInterconnect instance.

  • interconnectBGPInstance* object #

    BGP Service Instance parameters for the Interconnect.

    • controlPlane* string #

      Control plane protocol used for the service instance.

      enum: "EVPN", "IPVPN"
    • domainId string #

      D-PATH Domain ID used for the service instance.

    • encapsulation* string #

      Encapsulation type used for the service instance.

      enum: "VXLAN", "MPLS"
    • evi integer #

      Optional for IPVPN (can be used to auto-derive RTs), required for EVPN control plane on SRLinux.

      format: int32
    • exportPolicy string #

      Reference to a Policy CR that will be used to export routes.

    • exportTarget string #

      Export route target in 'target:N:N' format. One of ExportTarget or ExportPolicy must be specified.

    • importPolicy string #

      Reference to a Policy CR that will be used to import routes.

    • importTarget string #

      Import route target in 'target:N:N' format. One of ImportTarget or ImportPolicy must be specified.

    • mpls object #

      MPLS encapsulation options. Only applicable if MPLS encapsulation is used.

      • allowedTunnelTypes array[string] #

        Allowed tunnel types for MPLS transport.

    • routeDistinguisher* object #

      Route Distinguisher for the service instance.

      • assignedNumber string #

        // Administrator part of the route distinguisher (2- or 4-byte integer for Type 0 or Type 2 RDs, 4-byte dotted quad notation for Type 1 RDs). `SystemIP` keyword can be used to auto-assign with Type 1 RD.` // +eda:ui:title="Administrator" // +kubebuilder:validation:Optional Administrator string `json:"administrator,omitempty"` Assigned number part of the route distinguisher, or a `Pool` keyword to allocate from a pool.

      • rdPool string #

        Reference to a pool to use for Route Distinguisher allocation.

      • type string #

        Route distinguisher type.

        enum: "Type 0", "Type 1", "Type 2"
    • vxlan object #

      VXLAN encapsulation options. Only applicable if VXLAN encapsulation is used.

      • tunnelIndexPool string #

        Reference to tunnel index allocation pool.

        default: "tunnel-index-pool"
      • vni integer #

        VNI for the service instance.

        format: int32range: 1 to 16777215
  • nodeSelectors array[string] #

    Label selector to select nodes on which to configure the RouterInterconnect.

  • nodes array[string] #

    List of nodes (gateways) on which to configure the RouterInterconnect.

  • primaryBGPInstance object #

    Additional BGP Service Instance parameters related to the primary service instance (configured in the referenced Router).

    • domainId string #

      D-PATH Domain ID used for the service instance.

  • routerRef* object #

    Reference to the Router instance to interconnect.

    • name* string #

      Name of the Router instance.

    • namespace string #

      Namespace of the Router instance, if different from the referencing RouterInterconnect namespace.

STATUS

RouterInterconnectStatus defines the observed state of RouterInterconnect

  • health integer #

    Indicates the health score of the RouterInterconnect.

  • healthScoreReason string #

    Explains the reason for the health score.

  • lastChangeTime string #

    Timestamp of the last state change.

  • nodes array[string] #

    List of nodes on which the RouterInterconnect is deployed.

  • operationalState string #

    Operational state of the RouterInterconnect.

    enum: "Up", "Down", "Degraded", "Unknown"

docs.title: Custom Resource Definition
docs.text:
To browse the Custom Resource Definition go to crd.eda.dev.

VirtualNetwork

services.eda.nokia.com / v2

Expand All

SPEC

VirtualNetworkSpec defines the desired state of VirtualNetwork

  • bridgeDomains array[object] #

    List of Subnets. [emits=BridgeDomain]

    • [] object #
      • name* string #

        The name of the BridgeDomain.

      • spec* object #

        Specification of the BridgeDomain

        • configuredName string #

          The name of the BridgeDomain to configure on the device.

        • description string #

          The description of the BridgeDomain.

        • encapOptions object #

          Encapsulation-specific options (such as MPLS tunneling options).

          default: {"vxlan": {"tunnelIndexPool": "tunnel-index-pool", "vniPool": "vni-pool"}}
          • mpls object #

            MPLS encapsulation options. Only applicable if MPLS encapsulation is used (Type=EVPNMPLS).

            • allowedTunnelTypes array[string] #

              Allowed tunnel types for MPLS transport.

            • controlWord boolean #

              Enable or disable the use of control word. Applicable only to EVPNMPLS service Type.

          • vxlan object #

            VXLAN encapsulation options. Only applicable if VXLAN encapsulation is used (Type=EVPNVXLAN).

            • tunnelIndexPool string #

              Reference to a tunnel index pool to use for allocations.

              default: "tunnel-index-pool"
            • vni integer #

              VNI to use for this BridgeDomain, can be optionally left blank to have it allocated using the VNI Pool.

              range: 1 to 16777215
            • vniPool string #

              Reference to a VNI pool to use for allocations if VNI is left blank.

              default: "vni-pool"
        • evi integer #

          EVI to use for this BridgeDomain, can be optionally left blank to have it automatically allocated using the EVI Pool.

          range: 1 to 65535
        • eviPool string #

          Reference to an EVI pool to use for allocations if EVI is left blank.

          default: "evi-pool"
        • exportTarget string #

          Export route target in 'target:N:N' format, if not specified, the default value taken as "target:1:<evi>".

        • importTarget string #

          Import route target in 'target:N:N' format, if not specified, the default value taken as "target:1:<evi>".

        • l2proxyARPND object #

                When set to true, the global ASN value is prepended to the AS path in outbound routes towards each BGP peer.

                range: 1 to 255

          default: {"vxlan": {"tunnelIndexPool": "tunnel-index-pool", "vniPool": "vni-pool"}}
          • mpls object #

            MPLS encapsulation optionss. Only applicable if MPLS encapsulation is used (Type=EVPNMPLS or Type=IPVPN).

            • allowedTunnelTypes array[string] #

              Allowed tunnel types for MPLS transport.

          • vxlan object #

            VXLAN encapsulation options. Only applicable if VXLAN encapsulation is used (Type=EVPNVXLAN).

            • tunnelIndexPool string #

              Reference to tunnel index allocation pool.

              default: "tunnel-index-pool"
            • vni integer #

              VNI for the Router; leave blank for auto-allocation from VNI pool. Only applicable to VXLAN encapsulation.

              range: 1 to 16777215
            • vniPool string #

              Reference to VNI pool for auto-allocation. Only applicable to VXLAN encapsulation.

              default: "vni-pool"
        • evi integer #

          EVI for the Router; leave blank for auto-allocation from EVI pool. Optional for IPVPN (can be used to auto-derive RTs), required for EVPN.

          range: 1 to 65535
        • eviPool string #

          Reference to EVI pool for auto-allocation.

          default: "evi-pool"
        • exportTarget string #

          Export route target in 'target:N:N' format, if not specified, the default value taken as "target:1:<evi>".

        • importTarget string #

          Import route target in 'target:N:N' format, if not specified, the default value taken as "target:1:<evi>".

        • nodeSelectors array[string] #

          Node selectors for deployment constraints. If Nodes are selected, the Router will only be deployed on the Nodes selected, if left blank it will be deployed on all Nodes for which there are IRB or RoutedInterfaces referencing this Router.

        • resilientHashing object #

          Resilient Hashing configuration.

          • prefixes array[object] #

            IPv4 or IPv6 prefix. Active routes in the FIB that exactly match this prefix or that are longer matches of this prefix are provided with resilient-hash programming.

            • [] object #
              • hashBucketsPerPath* integer #

                The number of times each next-hop is repeated in the fill pattern if there are max-paths ECMP next-hops.

                default: 1range: 1 to 32
              • maxECMP* integer #

                The maximum number of ECMP next-hops per route associated with the resilient-hash prefix.

                default: 1range: 1 to 64
              • prefix* string #

                IPv4 or IPv6 prefix. Active routes in the FIB that exactly match this prefix or that are longer matches of this prefix are provided with resilient-hash programming.

        • routeLeaking object #

          Route leaking controlled by routing policies in and out of the DefaultRouter.

          • exportPolicy string #

            Reference to a Policy resource to use when evaluating route exports from the DefaultRouter.

          • importPolicy string #

            Reference to a Policy resource to use when evaluating route imports into the DefaultRouter.

        • routerID string #

          Router ID.

        • type string #

          Select the type of Router. Simple doesn't include any overlay control plane or dataplane properties (such as EVPN/VXLAN). EVPNVXLAN includes the properties needed to provision this Router over an IP Fabric.

          default: "EVPNVXLAN"enum: "Simple", "EVPNVXLAN", "EVPNMPLS", "IPVPN"
  • vlans array[object] #

    List of VLANs. [emits=VLAN]

    • [] object #
      • name* string #

        The name of the VLAN.

      • spec* object #

        Specification of the Vlan

        • bridgeDomain* string #

          Reference to a BridgeDomain or SimpleBridgeDomain.

        • description string #

          The description of the VLAN.

        • egress object #

          Manages actions on traffic at Egress.

          • filters array[string] #

            List of Filter references to use at egress.

          • qosPolicies array[string] #

            List of QoS Egress policy references to use at egress.

        • ingress object #

          Manages actions on traffic at Ingress.

          • filters array[string] #

            List of Filter references to use at ingress.

          • qosPolicies array[string] #

            List of QoS Ingress policy references to use at ingress.

        • interfaceSelectors* array[string] #

          Interfaces to use for attachment to this VLAN based on the label selector. Selects Interfaces based on their associated labels.

        • l2MTU integer #

          L2 MTU specifies the maximum sized Ethernet frame that can be transmitted on the subinterface. If a frame exceeds this size it is discarded. If the l2-mtu of the subinterface exceeds the port-mtu of the associated interface, the subinterface will remain operationally down.

          range: 1450 to 9500
        • macDuplicationDetectionAction string #

          If Mac Duplication Detection is enabled on the associated Bridge Domain, this property will override the MDD action set in the BridgeDomain.

          enum: "Blackhole", "OperDown", "StopLearning", "BridgeDomainAction"
        • splitHorizonGroup string #

          Name of the Split Horizon Group to be used for this VLAN. All subinterfaces within this VLAN will be members of this Split Horizon Group.

        • uplink object #

          The Uplink between your access breakout switch and your leaf switch.

          • egress object #

            Manages actions on traffic at Egress of the Local endpoint of the Uplink.

            • filters array[string] #

              List of Filter references to use at egress.

            • qosPolicies array[string] #

              List of QoS Egress policy references to use at egress.

          • ingress object #

            Manages actions on traffic at Ingress of the Local endpoint of the Uplink.

            • filters array[string] #

              List of Filter references to use at ingress.

            • qosPolicies array[string] #

              List of QoS Ingress policy references to use at ingress.

          • uplinkSelectors array[string] #

            Selects TopoLinks which connect a leaf switch to a breakout switch. This is the uplink between your access breakout switch and your leaf switch. There can only be a single TopoLink between the access breakout switch and the leaf switch, if more than one TopoLink is present between two devices the transaction will fail.

          • uplinkVLANID string #

            The VLAN ID to be utilized to isolate traffic from the VLAN on the access breakout switch to the leaf switch on the selected uplink TopoLink.

            default: "pool"
          • uplinkVLANPool string #

            A VLAN from this pool will be utilized to isolate traffic from the VLAN on the access breakout switch to the leaf switch on the selected uplink TopoLink.

        • vlanID string #

          Single value between 1-4094 support, ranges supported in the format x-y,x-y, or the special keyword null, any, untagged or pool for auto allocation.

          default: "pool"
        • vlanPool string #

          Reference to a VLAN pool to use for allocations. [default="vlan-pool"]

STATUS

  • health integer #

    Indicates the health score of the VNET.

  • healthScoreReason string #

    Indicates the reason for the health score.

  • lastChangeTime string #

    The time when the state of the resource last changed.

  • nodes array[string] #

    List of Nodes on which the VNET is configured.

  • numBGPPeers integer #

    Total number of configured BGP Peers.

  • numBGPPeersOperDown integer #

    Total Number of BGP Peer operationally down.

  • numIRBInterfaces integer #

    Total number of irb-interfaces configured by the VNET.

    format: int32
  • numIRBInterfacesOperDown integer #

    Total number of irb-interfaces configured by the VNET which are oper-down.

    format: int32
  • numNodes integer #

    Total number of Nodes on which the VNET is configured.

  • numOSPFInterfaces integer #

    Total number of configured OSPF Interfaces.

  • numOSPFInterfacesOperDown integer #

    Total Number of OSPF Interface operationally down.

  • numRoutedInterfaces integer #

    Total number of routed-interfaces configured by the VNET.

    format: int32
  • numRoutedInterfacesOperDown integer #

    Total number of routed-interfaces configured by the VNET which are oper-down.

    format: int32
  • numSubinterfaces integer #

    Total number of sub-interfaces configured by the VNET.

    format: int32
  • numSubinterfacesOperDown integer #

    Total number of sub-interfaces configured by the VNET which are oper-down.

    format: int32
  • operationalState string #

    Operational state of the VNET.

    enum: "Up", "Down", "Degraded", "Unknown"

VLAN

services.eda.nokia.com / v2

Expand All

SPEC

The VLAN enables the configuration and management of VLAN and their association with BridgeDomains. This resource allows for specifying the associated BridgeDomain, selecting interfaces based on label selectors, and configuring VLAN IDs with options for auto-allocation from a VLAN pool. It also supports advanced configurations such as ingress and egress traffic management, and overrides for MAC Duplication Detection actions when enabled in the associated BridgeDomain.

  • bridgeDomain* string #

    Reference to a BridgeDomain or SimpleBridgeDomain.

  • description string #

    The description of the VLAN.

  • egress object #

    Manages actions on traffic at Egress.

    • filters array[string] #

      List of Filter references to use at egress.

    • qosPolicies array[string] #

      List of QoS Egress policy references to use at egress.

  • ingress object #

    Manages actions on traffic at Ingress.

    • filters array[string] #

      List of Filter references to use at ingress.

    • qosPolicies array[string] #

      List of QoS Ingress policy references to use at ingress.

  • interfaceSelectors* array[string] #

    Interfaces to use for attachment to this VLAN based on the label selector. Selects Interfaces based on their associated labels.

  • l2MTU integer #

    L2 MTU specifies the maximum sized Ethernet frame that can be transmitted on the subinterface. If a frame exceeds this size it is discarded. If the l2-mtu of the subinterface exceeds the port-mtu of the associated interface, the subinterface will remain operationally down.

    range: 1450 to 9500
  • macDuplicationDetectionAction string #

    If Mac Duplication Detection is enabled on the associated Bridge Domain, this property will override the MDD action set in the BridgeDomain.

    enum: "Blackhole", "OperDown", "StopLearning", "BridgeDomainAction"
  • splitHorizonGroup string #

    Name of the Split Horizon Group to be used for this VLAN. All subinterfaces within this VLAN will be members of this Split Horizon Group.

  • uplink object #

    The Uplink between your access breakout switch and your leaf switch.

    • egress object #

      Manages actions on traffic at Egress of the Local endpoint of the Uplink.

      • filters array[string] #

        List of Filter references to use at egress.

      • qosPolicies array[string] #

        List of QoS Egress policy references to use at egress.

    • ingress object #

      Manages actions on traffic at Ingress of the Local endpoint of the Uplink.

      • filters array[string] #

        List of Filter references to use at ingress.

      • qosPolicies array[string] #

        List of QoS Ingress policy references to use at ingress.

    • uplinkSelectors array[string] #

      Selects TopoLinks which connect a leaf switch to a breakout switch. This is the uplink between your access breakout switch and your leaf switch. There can only be a single TopoLink between the access breakout switch and the leaf switch, if more than one TopoLink is present between two devices the transaction will fail.

    • uplinkVLANID string #

      The VLAN ID to be utilized to isolate traffic from the VLAN on the access breakout switch to the leaf switch on the selected uplink TopoLink.

      default: "pool"
    • uplinkVLANPool string #

      A VLAN from this pool will be utilized to isolate traffic from the VLAN on the access breakout switch to the leaf switch on the selected uplink TopoLink.

  • vlanID string #

    Single value between 1-4094 support, ranges supported in the format x-y,x-y, or the special keyword null, any, untagged or pool for auto allocation.

    default: "pool"
  • vlanPool string #

    Reference to a VLAN pool to use for allocations. [default="vlan-pool"]

STATUS

  • health integer #

    Indicates the health score of the VNET.

  • healthScoreReason string #

    Indicates the reason for the health score.

  • lastChangeTime string #

    The time when the state of the resource last changed.

  • numSubinterfaces integer #

    Total number of sub-interfaces configured by the VNET.

    format: int32
  • numSubinterfacesOperDown integer #

    Total number of sub-interfaces configured by the VNET which are oper-down.

    format: int32
  • operationalState string #

    Operational state of the VNET.

    enum: "Up", "Down", "Degraded", "Unknown"
  • subinterfaces array[object] #

    List of members in this Interface.

    • [] object #
      • enabled boolean #

        The administrative status of the SubInterface.

      • interface* string #

        Normalized interface name.

      • interfaceResource* string #

        Eda interface resource.

      • lastChangeTime string #

        Indicates when this SubInterface last changed state.

      • node* string #

        Reference to Node object.

      • nodeInterface* string #

        Node specific interface name.

      • operatingSystem string #

        Operating System of the Node.

      • operationalState string #

        Indicates the current operational state of the SubInterface.

        enum: "Up", "Down", "Degraded", "Unknown"
      • subinterfaceIndex integer #

        Index allocated to the subinterface on the associated interface for the given VLAN.

      • vlanID string #

        vlan assigned to this subinterface.

  • uplinks array[object] #

    List of uplinks in this Interface.

    • [] object #
      • enabled boolean #

        The administrative status of the SubInterface.

      • interface* string #

        Normalized interface name.

      • interfaceResource* string #

        Eda interface resource.

      • lastChangeTime string #

        Indicates when this SubInterface last changed state.

      • node* string #

        Reference to Node object.

      • nodeInterface* string #

        Node specific interface name.

      • operatingSystem string #

        Operating System of the Node.

      • operationalState string #

        Indicates the current operational state of the SubInterface.

        enum: "Up", "Down", "Degraded", "Unknown"
      • subinterfaceIndex integer #

        Index allocated to the subinterface on the associated interface for the given VLAN.

      • vlanID string #

        vlan assigned to this subinterface.

Banner

siteinfo.eda.nokia.com / v1

Expand All

SPEC

BannerSpec allows the configuration of login and MOTD (Message of the Day) banners on selected nodes. The banners can be applied to specific nodes or selected using label selectors.

  • loginBanner string #

    This is the login banner displayed before a user has logged into the Node.

  • motd string #

    This is the MOTD banner displayed after a user has logged into the Node.

  • nodeSelectors array[string] #

    Labe selector to select nodes on which to configure the banners.

  • nodes array[string] #

    List of nodes on which to configure the banners.

STATUS

BannerStatus defines the observed state of Banner

  • nodes array[string] #

    List of nodes this banner has been applied to

DefaultMTUSpec defines the desired state of DefaultMTU

  • interfaceMTU integer #

    Configures the Default MTU value for Ethernet interfaces. Includes Ethernet headers but excludes 4-byte FCS trailer.

    range: 1500 to 11000
  • layer2SubinterfaceMTU integer #

    Configures the Default MTU value for Layer 2 (bridged) interfaces. Includes Ethernet headers but excludes 4-byte FCS trailer.

    range: 1500 to 11000
  • layer3MTU integer #

    Configures the Default IP MTU value for Layer 3 interfaces. Includes IP headers but excludes Ethernet headers.

    range: 1280 to 11000
  • nodeSelectors array[string] #

    Label selector to select nodes on which to configure the defaults.

  • nodes array[string] #

    List of nodes on which to configure the defaults.

SystemLoadBalancer

siteinfo.eda.nokia.com / v1

Expand All

SPEC

SystemLoadBalancerSpec defines the desired state of SystemLoadBalancer

  • nodeSelectors array[string] #

    Label selector to select nodes on which to configure the parameters.

  • nodes array[string] #

    List of nodes on which to configure the parameteers.

  • srlinux object #

    SR Linux Load Balancer configuration.

    • dynamicLoadBalancing object #

      Dynamic Load Balancer configuration.

      • flowsetSize integer #

        The number of flowset entries reserved for each aggregate ECMP group.

        enum: 256, 512, 1024, +5 more
      • inactivityTimerUs integer #

        The flow inactivity timer in microseconds.

      • mode string #

        The dynamic load balancing mode.

        enum: "Dynamic", "Fixed", "PerPacket"
      • samplingIntervalUs integer #

        The sampling interval of interface state, in microseconds.

      • weighting object #

        Weighting factors for dynamic load balancing.

        • ingressTrafficManagerUtilizationPercent integer #

          Weight assigned to ingress Traffic Manager utilization.

          range: 0 to 100
        • portUtilizationPercent integer #

          Weight assigned to port utilization.

          range: 0 to 100
        • queueUtilizationPercent integer #

          Weight assigned to queue utilization.

          range: 0 to 100
    • hashOptions object #

      Hashing options.

      • destinationIP boolean #

        Include destination IP address in hash calculation.

      • destinationPort boolean #

        Include destination port in hash calculation.

      • flowLabel boolean #

        Include IPV6 Flow Label in hash calculation.

      • labelStack boolean #

        Include MPLS Label Stack in hash calculation.

      • protocol boolean #

        Include IP protocol in hash calculation.

      • sourceIP boolean #

        Include source IP address in hash calculation.

      • sourcePort boolean #

        Include source port in hash calculation.

      • vlanID boolean #

        Include VLAN ID in hash calculation.

  • sros object #

    SROS Load Balancer configuration.

    • hashOptions object #

      Hashing options.

      • enhancedELER boolean #

        Enables Enhanced eLER Load Balancing.

      • enhancedMulticast boolean #

        Enables Enhanced Multicast Load Balancing.

      • layer4 boolean #

        Enables Layer 4 Load Balancing.

      • lsrMode string #

        Defines load balancing more for LSR role.

        enum: "LabelOnly", "LabelIP", "IPOnly", +4 more
      • serviceID boolean #

        Enables VLL Service ID Load Balancing.

      • systemIP boolean #

        Enables use of the System IP address as a hashing input.

STATUS

SystemLoadBalancerStatus defines the observed state of SystemLoadBalancer

  • srlinuxNodes array[string] #

    List of nodes this CR has been applied to

  • srosNodes array[string] #

NTPClient

timing.eda.nokia.com / v1

Expand All

SPEC

The NTP client allows for configuring NTP servers and the source of NTP traffic in order for the devices to synchronize their clocks.

  • router string #

    Router used to reach the NTP servers.

  • routerKind* string #

    the Kind of the router used to reach the NTP servers.

    enum: "ManagementRouter", "Router", "DefaultRouter"
  • routerSelectors array[string] #

    Selects router resources based on the defined KIND. Applies to DefaultRouter only. Not supported for Router and ManagementRouter.

  • servers* array[object] #

    A list of NTP servers, each entry in the list can either be an IP address or an FQDN.

    • [] object #
      • iBurst boolean #

        Indicates whether this server should enable burst synchronization or not

      • preferred boolean #

        Indicates whether this server should be preferred or not

      • server* string #

        An NTP server can either be an IP address or an FQDN

  • sourceInterface string #

    Specifies a Interface resource to use as a source of NTP traffic. If none is specified the Node default behavior is used.

  • sourceInterfaceKind string #

    Specifies the source interface Kind to use as a source of NTP traffic.

    enum: "IRBInterface", "RoutedInterface", "DefaultInterface", "SystemInterface"

STATUS

NTPClientStatus defines the observed state of NTPClient

  • health integer #

    Indicates the health score of the NTPClient

  • healthScoreReason string #

    Indicates the reason for the health score

  • lastChangeTime string #

    The time when the state of the resource last changed

  • nodes array[object] #

    List of nodes which are not synchronized

    • [] object #
      • node* string #

        Reference to Node object

      • operatingSystem* string #

        Operating System of the Node

      • synchronized string #

        Synchronized state of the Node

  • operationalState string #

    Operational state of the NTPClient

    enum: "Up", "Down", "Degraded", "Unknown"
